result:
All green on a clean build: 62 tests, zero warnings. Work complete — final summary.

PART 1 — both falsified R1 laws corrected, oracle-pinned, and regression-locked:

1a. EQUATION ORDERING. The old byte-sort law is replaced in `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/workspace/pretty-clean/src/signature.rs` by a structural order on the (lhs, rhs) pair, pinned by 13 new probes (p_eqA–p_eqI, workspace/scratchpad/probes/): declaration order is irrelevant in both directions (p_eqA/B, p_eqF/F2 identical echoes); variables rank below ALL applications (p_eqC/C2: `f(zzz,…)` before nullary `f(a0,…)`); var-vs-var compares name bytes then index (p_eqD azz<b refutes shortlex; p_eqH x<x.1); app-vs-app compares head-name bytes then arguments left-to-right (p_eqG refutes arity-first); tuples compare as RIGHT-NESTED binary `pair` applications (p_eqE g<pair, p_eqG pair<z1, p_eqI `<x, zz>` < `<x, b, c>`); rhs breaks lhs ties (p_eqF). Reproduces both counter-examples byte-exactly.

1b. WIDE-TUPLE WRAP. `pair_doc` in `src/term.rs` is now one fcat whose items are `<`, each element (attached `", "`) under nest 1, and `>`. Probe p_pw1 pinned all four shapes (fits; elements fill at col(`<`)+1; `<` alone when the first element's one-liner doesn't fit beside it; `>` beside the last element unless that element is multi-line, then alone at col(`<`)); mesh k2 reproduces byte-exactly, including trailing-space fill lines.

Acceptance: whole-signature-block parity now asserted for 12 files — the 10 round-1 targets PLUS contract.spthy and mesh.spthy, materialized via the oracle into `round1/targets/` (verified identical to canonical-flag runs).

PART 2 — R2 rule rendering implemented in `src/rule.rs` (+ `src/ast.rs` extensions, `src/macros.rs` for the macros surface, `src/lib.rs` entry points `render_rule`/`render_fact`). Curated 12-file round created at `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/round2/` (families.tsv, fetch script, targets). Acceptance met: all 72 rule blocks across the 12 captures render byte-identically (`tests/round2_rules.rs` — a layout-insensitive echo-parser round-trips every block, so layout can only come from the renderer; plus 9 hand-fixture probe-pinned unit tests). Laws pinned by probes p_lb2/p_fann/p_var1/p_rattr/p_mac1/p_lbvar/p_arr1 + corpus witnesses, all documented in BEHAVIOR.md "Rule blocks (R2)": nested-sep bracket groups (one construction for `[ ]` and `--[ ]->`, three graded shapes, boundaries at exactly ribbon 73), true-sep body with out-dented arrow, fact layout + annotation canonical order `[+, -, no_precomp]`, attribute canonical order `color/no_derivcheck/issapicrule/role` with drops and last-wins, loop-breaker singular/plural and inside-comment placement, variants comment with right-aligned index field (1/2/3-digit witnesses), substitution `lhs $$ nest 6 ("= " <> rhs)` overlap layout, 4-space group separator lines, macros block. Unobserved surfaces (embedded _restrict, diff-mode left/right, theory-level looping-facts comment) are explicitly flagged, not guessed.

State: `cargo test` = 62 passed / 0 failed, zero warnings, std-only + vendored BSD Doc engine. BEHAVIOR.md corrected (wrong laws struck with refutation provenance) and extended; QUERIES.log records every oracle interaction (one line each; ≤6 concurrent; OOM-guarded scripts untouched). No files outside /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/ modified; no commits.

Round-2 similarity audit complete. Appended to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/pretty/AUDIT.md`.

Summary of what I verified against upstream (`tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover/`):

- **Equation-ORDERING law (special scrutiny) — derivation is probe/corpus provenance, not source-reading.** Upstream order is the derived `Ord CtxtStRule` via `S.toList (stRules)` (Signature.hs 226). The clean law traces to (a) logged corpus counter-examples `contract.echo`/`mesh.echo` that *refuted* the round-1 byte-order guess (checkpcs `xpk` before `pk(xsk)` though 'x'>'p'; `cnf` before `aes_cmac`), materialized as green byte targets, and (b) nine discriminating reorder probes p_eqA–p_eqI (most with declaration-swap controls), including p_eqI which empirically separates right-nested-binary from flattened tuple comparison. Decisively, the law **diverges from the source `Ord` in every non-observable slot**: upstream `Ord LVar` is idx→sort→name (LTerm.hs 522) but the clean is name→idx with no sort; upstream breaks lhs ties on `StRhs`'s `[Position]` list first but the clean goes straight to rhs; the clean invents `CmpView::PairTail` to re-binarize its n-ary `Pair` node, a device upstream (binary pairs) has no analogue for. A transcriber copies idx-sort-name and the position field; these omissions are affirmative non-transcription evidence.

- **Tuple-wrap correction (`pair_doc`)** now equals upstream's `ppTerms ", " 1 "<" ">"` form, but it is byte-forced by the four p_pw1 shapes (the `<`-alone shape forces `<` as a breakable fcat item; continuation forces `nest 1`; `>`-at-origin forces unnested `>`), was reached by iterating from a logged wrong R1 guess, and AC operators were pointedly *not* unified onto the same helper (upstream shares one `ppTerms`). Merger.

- **R2 rule rendering** — body nest uses the same +3/−1 vs upstream +2/+1 re-encoding fingerprint seen at R1; bracket groups are a nested all-or-nothing `sep` (own shared helper) vs upstream's `fsep` fill; facts use `<+>` beside vs upstream's `nestShort'`/`$$` overlap; substitution `lhs $$ nest 6 (= rhs)` equals upstream but the overlap-padding is byte-forced (p_var1) and the numbering is inlined with no `numbered'`/`flushRight` chain; macros diverge in tokenization. The clean reaches BSD `$+$` (`above_plus`) directly, never the GPL wrapper's `$-$` alias.

- **Scans:** identifier-leakage grep clean (only the English word "numbered"); `doc.rs`/`Cargo.toml` untouched; 47/47 round-1 (incl. new order + tuple tests) and 9/9 round-2 parity tests pass here (72 corpus rule blocks, layout-insensitive echo-parser so layout can only come from the renderer).

No copied protectable expression. Two non-blocking correctness caveats recorded (LVar-key and `[Position]` omissions the full-corpus gate must backstop) — these are the non-transcription evidence, not similarity findings.

VERDICT: pass
